100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 1, 1926, the United States celebrated the opening of its first coast-to-coast radio network, revolutionizing communication and entertainment. Internationally, tensions rose in Europe as political unrest continued in Germany following the aftermath of World War I. Meanwhile, in the Middle East, the British mandate over Palestine faced increasing challenges from local populations. The world remained on edge as economic recovery fluctuated in the post-war landscape.
